question: Which inbox makes you sweat most?

options.label: The avalanche
options.imagePrompt: Hand-drawn illustration in soft pastel colors, cozy and warm style. Soft watercolor texture, gentle hand-painted lines, flat illustration. Palette: lavender, cream, sage green, blush pink. Calm, friendly, approachable, non-judgmental mood. No people, no faces, no text, no letters. Vertical 9:16 composition. A cozy desktop computer screen glowing softly, with a towering stack of envelopes overflowing in a cascade around the keyboard, envelopes spilling onto the desk surface, a small potted succulent nearly buried under the pile, warm lamplight casting gentle shadows.
options.result: Avoidance is your default - unseen tasks feel like threats your brain would rather not process.

options.label: The one unread
options.imagePrompt: Hand-drawn illustration in soft pastel colors, cozy and warm style. Soft watercolor texture, gentle hand-painted lines, flat illustration. Palette: lavender, cream, sage green, blush pink. Calm, friendly, approachable, non-judgmental mood. No people, no faces, no text, no letters. Vertical 9:16 composition. A cozy desktop computer screen glowing softly, almost completely tidy inbox scene with just a single small envelope resting alone in the center of the screen, surrounded by empty space, a cup of tea steaming beside the monitor, a clean minimal desk.
options.result: Your brain fixates hard - one loose end can hijack your entire day until it is closed.

options.label: The color coded
options.imagePrompt: Hand-drawn illustration in soft pastel colors, cozy and warm style. Soft watercolor texture, gentle hand-painted lines, flat illustration. Palette: lavender, cream, sage green, blush pink. Calm, friendly, approachable, non-judgmental mood. No people, no faces, no text, no letters. Vertical 9:16 composition. A cozy desktop computer screen glowing softly, showing a neatly organized collection of small folders in different soft pastel shades arranged in careful rows on the screen, colored sticky note flags on the monitor edges, a ruler and highlighters on the desk beside it, everything symmetrical and deliberate.
options.result: You build perfect systems to feel in control - but the system itself becomes the procrastination.

options.label: What inbox
options.imagePrompt: Hand-drawn illustration in soft pastel colors, cozy and warm style. Soft watercolor texture, gentle hand-painted lines, flat illustration. Palette: lavender, cream, sage green, blush pink. Calm, friendly, approachable, non-judgmental mood. No people, no faces, no text, no letters. Vertical 9:16 composition. A cozy desktop computer screen sitting dark and switched off, a thin layer of dust suggested on the screen surface, a forgotten mug with a dried tea ring beside it, a closed notebook and a single pen resting on top, soft ambient light from a nearby window, peaceful but neglected.
options.result: Out of sight is completely out of mind - if you can not see it, your brain acts like it does not exist.

introVoiceover: Four inboxes. One of them made your stomach drop. Pick the one that hits hardest - because your choice says something real about how your ADHD brain handles stress. Comment your answer below.
outroVoiceover: None of these are wrong - they are just your brain doing its thing. Follow for daily ADHD content.
title: Which inbox gives you anxiety? (ADHD test) #Shorts
description:
Pick the inbox that stresses you out most - then watch as we reveal what each choice says about your ADHD brain. The answers are all in the video.
Follow for daily ADHD content.
